name: verify-plan-adversarial-framing
description: verify_plan.md title: 'Devil's Advocate Review of Story Coverage'. Explains QA only sees Story (not original prompt), this is last chance before context wipe. SM must challenge the draft, not rubber-stamp. Brain examples are SM-scoped: requirements/architecture/decisions.

content: All 6 core-steps have role-scoped Brain search examples: SM (review_previous_notes, draft_story, verify_plan) = requirements/architecture/decisions; QA (write_failing_tests, verify_green_state) = test naming/conventions/frameworks; DEV (implement_tasks) = code patterns/module structure/error handling.

bootstrap-handoff pattern: setup_prism_loop.py writes .prism/handoff.md after brain_bootstrap() only if no handoff exists. Minimal content: header, prompt, timestamp, note that no prior context exists. Ensures review_previous_notes always takes fast Path A.

name: review-previous-notes-token-budget
description: core-steps/review_previous_notes.md has explicit token budget (50K, max 3 files) and DO NOT guardrails: no codebase exploration, no Glob/Grep for patterns. Two paths: Path A (handoff exists) reads handoff only, Path B (no handoff) reads prompt + 1-2 recent stories + one Brain query. setup_prism_loop.py writes bootstrap handoff via resolve_handoff_file() so first runs also take the fast path.

content: review_previous_notes is pure context restoration: read handoff (always present), read story_file if named in handoff, summarize, STOP. Zero Glob/Grep/Brain. 10K token budget. No Path A/B branching.
